SourceRef: Datasheet.md > Identification > Objectives Supported; Guidance.md > Purpose

Statement: DEL-015-05 requires IFC Low-Voltage System Plans (DEL-004-07) before installation can proceed. DEL-004-07 defines routing quantities cable types and termination locations.
EvidenceQuote: IFC Low-Voltage System Plans (DEL-004-07) issued and P.Eng.-stamped
Notes: FACT. Explicit prerequisite P-01 in Procedure. Also referenced as Hold Point HP-01 gating Phase 2. Not yet issued per Procedure status. BLOCKING for estimating: cable types and quantities unknown without this document.

Statement: DEL-015-05 requires Electrical Specification (DEL-004-09) before installation. DEL-004-09 defines material and workmanship standards for low-voltage work.
Notes: FACT. Explicit prerequisite P-02 in Procedure. Not yet issued. BLOCKING for estimating: conduit type and workmanship standards unknown without this document.

Statement: DEL-015-05 must coordinate with DEL-015-04 for shared conduit pathways and penetrations. Separation distances per Alberta Electrical Code must be maintained.
SourceRef: Procedure.md > Prerequisites > Upstream Work Prerequisites > U-01; Specification.md > REQ-015-05-09
EvidenceQuote: Shared conduit pathways and penetrations should be coordinated
Notes: FACT. Explicit upstream work prerequisite U-01 in Procedure. REQ-015-05-09 mandates coordination. Procedure U-01 calls this 'Coordination' but REQ-015-05-09 and Specification mandate specific coordination of sleeves and separation distances -- classified as INTERFACE (shared physical pathway).

Statement: DEL-015-05 requires concrete pours and framing by PKG-011 to be coordinated so that conduit sleeves are embedded before pours and wall closures. Cannot retrofit through concrete without core drilling.
SourceRef: Procedure.md > Prerequisites > Upstream Work Prerequisites > U-02
EvidenceQuote: Low-voltage conduit sleeves must be embedded or roughed-in before pours and wall closures
Notes: FACT. Explicit hard predecessor U-02 in Procedure. Guidance Principle 1 reinforces: missed rough-in in concrete construction is expensive to remedy. TargetType=PACKAGE because multiple PKG-011 deliverables may be involved (superstructure DEL-011-01 and others).

TargetLocation: Alberta Safety Codes authority having jurisdiction (AHJ)
Statement: DEL-015-05 requires electrical permit from AHJ before installation. REQ-015-05-04 mandates Alberta Safety Code compliance.
SourceRef: Procedure.md > Prerequisites > Document Prerequisites > P-03; Specification.md > REQ-015-05-04
EvidenceQuote: Electrical Safety Code permit obtained from Alberta authority having jurisdiction
Notes: FACT. Explicit prerequisite P-03 in Procedure. REQ-015-05-04 in Specification. Step 1.4 details the permit process. External regulatory constraint.

TargetName: Contract scope boundary confirmation (CONF-015-05-01 and CONF-015-05-02)
Statement: DEL-015-05 requires confirmation of contract scope boundary (wiring/conduit only vs. equipment supply) before full planning and procurement. Unresolved scope boundary impacts cable type selection and procurement.
SourceRef: Procedure.md > Prerequisites > Document Prerequisites > P-04; Guidance.md > Conflict Table > CONF-015-05-01 and CONF-015-05-02
EvidenceQuote: Contract scope boundary confirmed: wiring/conduit only vs. equipment supply
Notes: FACT. Explicit prerequisite P-04 in Procedure referencing Guidance Conflict Table items CONF-015-05-01 and CONF-015-05-02. Resolution needed from Owner/Electrical Engineer. Impacts estimating: scope boundary determines whether active components are in scope.

Statement: DEL-015-05 may require control system design specifications from Owner if security or radio system specs are provided. These inform cable type selection.
SourceRef: Procedure.md > Prerequisites > Upstream Work Prerequisites > U-03
EvidenceQuote: If security or radio system specs are provided by Owner these inform cable type selection
Notes: FACT. Explicit information prerequisite U-03 in Procedure. Low confidence because dependency is conditional ('if provided by Owner'). May affect cable type selection for estimating.

Statement: DEL-004-07 IFC drawings must be P.Eng.-stamped before DEL-015-05 construction commences. REQ-015-05-05 explicitly constrains start of installation.
EvidenceQuote: All IFC drawings shall be signed and stamped by a professional engineer licensed to practice in Alberta
Notes: FACT. Explicit requirement REQ-015-05-05 in Specification. Distinct from DEP-015-05-006 because this captures the P.Eng. stamp as a specific approval constraint rather than the document content prerequisite.
